text:
In the first space, I sense a curious anticipation—a place both open and slightly guarded, imbued with the silent potential of an event yet to come. The starkness of the environment, softened by the warm lights, makes me feel like a spectator pausing at the threshold between the mundane and the extraordinary.

Moving into the second scene, the vibrant colors and bold text spark my curiosity. Here, the world of ideas and innovation feels tactile and alive. I am acutely aware of myself as both a learner and a participant in a broader intellectual experiment, surrounded by knowledge in motion. It makes me reflect on the ever-shifting boundary between the observer and the observed, between thinking and doing. I feel both engaged and slightly displaced, as if I am standing on the edge of discovery.
